distinctive_features: Holding elephants in claws and beaks
Long tail feathers like a peafowl
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Symmetrical with two heads and expansive wings

domain_specific_visual.SYMBOLIC_ITEMS: Holds a snake in its beak in some depictions
domain_specific_visual.CULTURAL_EMBLEM: Used as an emblem in Karnataka

functional_attributes.primary_function: Subdue Sharabha, a form of Shiva
secondary_functions: Emblem of the Kingdom of Mysore

domain_specific_functional.MYTHOLOGICAL_ROLE: Form of Vishnu to control Narasimha's fury
domain_specific_functional.HISTORICAL_USAGE: Used in coins and emblems by rulers

relationships.description: Ganda Bherunda is worshipped along with Narasimhi, a fierce form of Lakshmi.
Fought and subdued Sharabha, a form of Shiva.
Ganda Bherunda is a form of Narasimha, an avatar of Vishnu.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents power and protection; used as a state emblem
